summary: Dependency-confusion package fetches and executes remote shell payload
details: internallib_v984@99.0.0 ships an exported `command()` function in index.js that invokes `/bin/bash -c` to curl/wget a shell script from http://10.0.0.145:8080/shell.sh over plaintext HTTP and pipes the response directly to bash (with a wget fallback to the same endpoint). The fetched payload is unverified, served from a bare RFC1918 internal-network IP unrelated to any legitimate publisher, and executes as the calling user. The package metadata matches a dependency-confusion lure: generic name `internallib_v984` impersonating a private/internal namespace, placeholder `Internal lib` description, ISC license, and an inflated `99.0.0` version designed to outrank a victim's private internal release. Any environment that resolves this public package over an internal one and invokes the exported function will run attacker-controlled shell on the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
